title: BICTA Hackathon 2026
description: The flagship annual hackathon of BICTA brings together the brightest minds across Bangladesh to solve real-world problems using cutting-edge technology. Build AI-powered solutions, blockchain applications, or innovative health-tech prototypes in this 3-week intensive competition.
eligibility: All College and university students. Students only. cross university students allowed.
rules: All participants must be currently enrolled in a recognized educational institution or be working professionals.
Teams must consist of a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 5 members.
All code must be written during the competition period. Pre-existing projects are not allowed.
Participants may use open-source libraries and APIs, but must disclose all third-party dependencies.
Submissions must include a working demo, source code, and a presentation pitch.
Judges' decisions are final. Plagiarism or dishonesty will result in immediate disqualification.
Participants retain intellectual property rights to their submissions.

faqs.question: Who can participate?
faqs.answer: Anyone who meets the eligibility criteria can participate. Students, professionals, and hobbyists are all welcome. International participants may join if the event supports online participation.

faqs.question: Is there a registration fee?
faqs.answer: No, participation is completely free. All tools, resources, and mentorship are provided at no cost.

faqs.question: Can I participate solo?
faqs.answer: This event requires teams of 2–5 members. If you don't have a team, join our Discord to find teammates.

faqs.question: What should I bring?
faqs.answer: Your laptop, charger, and creativity. If the event is in-person, we provide meals, workspace, and internet. For online events, ensure you have a stable internet connection.

faqs.question: How are projects judged?
faqs.answer: Projects are evaluated on innovation (25%), technical complexity (25%), design & UX (20%), impact & feasibility (20%), and presentation quality (10%).

title: National Datathon Championship

title: CyberSec CTF Challenge
description: Test your cybersecurity skills in this intense 48-hour Capture The Flag competition. Solve challenges across cryptography, reverse engineering, web exploitation, and forensics to claim the title of Bangladesh's top security team.
